label: Invasive Mussels' Impact on Great Lakes & Whitefish

comments.text:
#ResistanceEarth
#BlueEarth Whitefish have long been a part of Great Lakes cuisine and culture, but the iconic fish are disappearing. Invasive mussels have filtered away zooplankton that whitefish depend on. If the whitefish collapse, it will impact other species. www.bridgemi.com/michigan-env...

Scientists are fighting back against invasive zebra and quagga mussels in Lake Michigan. #906Life #mymichigan
www.jsonline.com/story/news/l...

This is devastating, how quagga mussels are single-handedly destroying the Great Lakes. Introduced by foreign ships that didn't empty their bilge before coming into the Great Lakes 🤬 #906Life #MyMichigan www.wpr.org/environment/...
A native Great Lakes whitefish thought extinct for nearly 40 years has been rediscovered by scientists — in the wrong Great Lake. #mymichigan www.usatoday.com/story/news/n...
A changing ecosystem is depleting the whitefish population in the Great Lakes

Lake Superior hasn’t yet succumbed to the invasive mussels that have destroyed ecosystems in the other Great Lakes. On a remote Michigan archipelago south of Thunder Bay, Ont., scientists are doing what they can to hold the line. Via .com thenarwhal.ca/lake-superio...

More great work by our Rangers today, intercepting a boat infested with invasive zebra mussels from Lake Ontario, NY, from entering our most critical lake for recreation and wildlife. Thanks to Colorado Parks & Wildlife for doing everything they can to prevent these invaders from clogging our lakes.

An old sketch of an Arctic grayling for an Arctic-themed #SundayFishSketch! Despite their name, this species was formerly found as far south as the #GreatLakes basin (see the Michigan city of Grayling), but habitat loss and invasive species led to the grayling's extirpation in the basin

Muskrats have adapted to eating the invasive zebra mussels found throughout the Great Lakes. Mother Nature always finds a way.
#Nikon #Photography #NaturePhotography #WildlifePhotography #Muskrats

In the lower #GreatLakes, whitefish numbers have been declining in part due to invasive mussels - now a bill under consideration in the Michigan state Senate would allocate funding to the state's DNR for a rearing/stocking program to support whitefish recovery www.greatlakesnow.org/2026/05/05/m...

Zebra mussels and quagga mussels are not actually closely related to true mussels. They were introduced to North America in the ballast water of ships and have completely changed nutrient chemistry and ecology of the Great Lakes through their filtering activity (55)
in buffalo, we have had an invasive species of mussels in lake erie for fucking decades. mussels that EAT THE HULLS OF BOATS and are not being sold as “food” in “restaurants”
Scientists wonder if Lake Superior can continue to avoid an invasion of mussels #GreatLakes www.mprnews.org/story/2025/0...

Golden mussels in California. Zebra mussels in the Great Lakes. Quagga mussels in Colorado. Tiny invaders, massive impacts—starving ecosystems, clogging infrastructure, and leaving collapse in their wake. www.sfgate.com/bayarea/arti...
All that white stuff on the beach in the foreground are the dead carcass of invasive quagga mussels. They are changing Lake Michigan and not for the better.
Whitefish are Disappearing. What Can We Do? #GreatLakes www.greatlakesnow.org/the-fight-to...

Invasive quagga and zebra mussels have disrupted the food web in parts of the Grest Lakes, diverting nutrients away from planktivorous fishes, thereby "making it extremely difficult for whitefish to reach adulthood in Lake Michigan and Huron".
www.michiganpublic.org/environment-...
As climate change and invasive species threaten the whitefish’s reproductive patterns, experts say the species is at risk of disappearing entirely from Lake Michigan in the next few years.

"Like lobster to Maine and salmon to Alaska, whitefish is one of the signature animals in Michigan's history" But after decades of slow decline, the fish are now on the brink of collapse in most of Lakes Michigan + Huron in part due to invasive mussels #GreatLakes www.bridgemi.com/michigan-env...

The devastation that this species has done to the Great Lakes, brought into the Lakes by international shippers who didn't bother to empty their polluted ballast and these people are responsible for destroying the whitefish fishery in Lake Michigan. These are quagga mussels. #906Life
Deer tracks amid the millions of dead quagga mussel shells that have washed up on my beach here on NW Lake Michigan. #906Life #PureMichigan
i will never forgive the culinary industry for trying to convince ppl zebra mussels are edible all of a sudden like they haven’t been eating the HULLS OF BOATS in lake erie for the last 35 years

Excited to be giving two talks today at the Indiana Invasive Species Conference in Northwest Indiana! Talking all things aquatic invasive species prevention as well as giving a brief history of zebra mussels in the #GreatLakes

Quagga mussels confirmed in Black Bay, marking latest invasive spread on Lake Superior Ministry of Natural Resources says mussels have been found on the bay’s north side www.cbc.ca/news/canada/... #GreatLakes

Some of the #GreatLakes most damaging invasive species - like zebra mussels + round gobies - were introduced via ballast water. However, ballast water management systems on ships have been shown to reduce the abundance of living organisms in ballast water by more than 98% www.wpr.org/news/conflic...
if zebra mussels were reliably edible, Aaron and I would have cleaned up the Great Lakes by now
Tribes move whitefish upriver in bid to revive Great Lakes runs Featuring .bsky.social #GreatLakes www.mlive.com/environment/...
We are truly doomed. Quadrillions of invasive mussels are damaging the Great Lakes, documentary shows. #mymichigan www.wpr.org/environment/...
The couple of days of strong East winds that we had brought a bunch of invasive quagga mussel shells on the shore. The next storm will wash them back out, Great Lakes life lol #906life #mymichigan

I love all bivalves. But I have to admit that zebra and quagga mussels are pretty sinister. They have completely re-engineered the Great Lakes since they invaded in the 80s via ship ballast water. They eat all the good food, block the sediment and even smother native bivalves! (334)

NOAA's National Mussel Watch Program - which has used invasive zebra + quagga mussels to track contaminants in the #GreatLakes since 1992 - finds that PFAS are ubiquitous in the lakes, with mussels in Lake Michigan having the highest average PFAS concentrations www.bridgemi.com/michigan-env...
Anyone want to buy some zebra or quagga mussel shells? Unfortunately, I have a near unlimited supply of these invasive Invaders to the Great Lakes. #906Life #mymichigan

Quagga mussels confirmed in Black Bay, marking latest invasive spread on Lake Superior #Ontario Ministry of Natural Resources says mussels have been found on the bay’s north side www.cbc.ca/news/canada/...

A recent study in Freshwater Science sheds light on the ability of muskrats to help manage aquatic invasive plants like invasive cattail and European frogbit in #GreatLakes wetlands bridgemi.com/michigan-env...

Invasive bloody red shrimp - first found in the #GreatLakes in 2006 - is now established in all of the lakes. The small crustacean is native to the Caspian/Black Sea and is thought to have arrived in North America via ballast water dumped by ocean-going cargo ships greatlakesecho.org/2026/06/07/s...

Whitefish is the highest value fishery in Lake Michigan (estimated value ~$2.8 million last year), but invasive zebra/quagga mussels have contributed to the population declining by 75% in the last 2 decades. Now, the Wisconsin DNR is proposing limiting commercial harvest www.wpr.org/news/dnr-lim...
Great Lakes Friends! New documentary with stunning underwater footage. And some hope for lake fauna despite quagga mussels. www.jsonline.com/story/sports...
Pretty wild water clarity in Manitowoc today. Quagga mussels have cleared up the water but a lot of time here we have waves stirring up the fine sediment.
The two invasive Great Lakes goby species for #SundayFishSketch. Round goby are widespread, while the tubenose goby still has a fairly limited distribution.

Whitefish Point hooks into Lake Superior from Michigan’s Upper Peninsula. The hook forms a bay to the east, providing shelter from wind and waves coming out of the northwest. Whitefish Bay has been the salvation of many ships trying to survive the worst of the lake’s November gales.
As I like to tell my kids, we’re the ultimate invasive species! The zebra mussels in the Great Lakes are amateurs.

Lake Superior has long resisted invasive mussels, but recent outbreaks at Isle Royale and beyond are testing the Big Lake’s defenses. Scientists race to stop quagga and zebra mussels from threatening native fish and fragile ecosystems. #WaterNews #GreatLakes #LakeSuperior
Quadrillions of Invasive Mussels Damaging the Great Lakes

The spread of quagga mussels in the Great Lakes are threatening fish stocks and historic wrecks. .bsky.social spoke with Yvonne Drebert and Zach Melnick about .bsky.social, their .bsky.social documentary about the invasive species.
omg I would CRY if there was an extra $500 million in research funds to combat invasive quagga mussels in the Great Lakes!

A 2017 New Yorker article compared cancer spread to a zebra mussel invasion. The author claims he was frustrated at me for failing to identify "a principal cause" for the Great Lakes invasion. If he had asked for that, I would've explained propagule pressure to him. www.newyorker.com/magazine/201...

Astounding underwater photos showing billions of invasive quagga mussels dominating the bottom of the Great Lakes. #bioinvasions
www.jsonline.com/picture-gall...

They are also revered here too by the nations on 🇨🇦's west coast. My nation's traditional territory is around the Great Lakes. Love Orcas but miss the fresh water. Unfortunately, invasive species from Asia like the zebra mussels & lamprey eels have infested the Great Lakes.🤬

Quagga and zebra (aka Dreissenid) mussels can have drastically different impacts on food webs depending on where they invade - not a "one size fits all" invader W Lake Erie --> increase in summer cyanobacteria/algae blooms S Lake Michigan --> disappearance of spring diatom bloom
A huge mound of quagga mussel shells on the shore of Lake Michigan (photo from an article in Discover magazine: www.discovermagazine.com/environment/...)
Lake Superior is the lone holdout in a mussel invasion that has overtaken every other Great Lake.

Earned a PhD (NSF); made models to predict invasive hydrilla in the Great Lakes (USACE); expanded undergraduate research at my Uni (USDA); discovered new populations of an endangered crayfish in TX (Section 6); developed early detection methods for invasive zebra mussels in TX reservoirs (USFWS)

Zebra + quagga mussels are small invasive organisms with a big impact on the #GreatLakes. Controlling them is a daunting challenge, but public outreach, regional partnerships, and ongoing research can make a difference in the Great Lakes and beyond. #NISAW

Join .bsky.social at .bsky.social on March 4 for a FREE 4K screening of #TVOOriginal “All Too Clear.” Filmmakers Zach Melnick & Yvonne Drebert spent 150+ days capturing how invasive quagga mussels are reshaping the Great Lakes. 🎟️Get tickets here: www.eventbrite.ca/e/tvo-presen...

Lake Whitefish are in the middle of a population crash in the Great Lakes.
#GreatLakes #fisheries #PureMichigan
www.bridgemi.com/michigan-env...
folkqueen is gonna hit the cingray anime onlys like zebra mussels in the great lakes
it is the same as the initiatives preventing invasive mussels in the great lakes. we were working so hard to stop it and they are going to cause SO MUCH damage.
The alewife die off in Lake Michigan - especially on the Green Bay side is stinky and terrible this year. Need more predators of those damn things
Quagga mussel veliger production, growth, and timing across a depth gradient in southeast Lake Michigan #GreatLakes 🧪 www.sciencedirect.com/science/arti...
My Lake Erie adjacency suggests: there is also the option of zebra mussels.
Wow, what an amazing new find of a 19th century shipwreck in Lake Ontario. I hate that invasive quagga mussels will be slowly tearing this apart in decades to come.

Native Lonicera sempervirens hasn't been aggressive in any of the locations I've planted it (I'm in the great lakes region). But it is important to get the straight species; non-native honeysuckles are often very aggressive and terribly invasive, unfortunately.

Fried, whipped into a pâté or dried and ground into powder in the Anishinaabe tradition, whitefish have been a cornerstone of Great Lakes cuisine and culture for millenia. After decades of decline, they're now on the brink of collapse. Story by .com:
www.greatlakesnow.org/2025/06/icon...

Hiring project coordinator - USFS research on Great Lakes freshwater mussels. 1 year w possible renewal, PhD / experience with mussels / spatial analytical skills preferred. Based at USFS Northern Research Station, Rhinelander WI. For more information/to apply, www.ugajobsearch.com/postings/438...

Watch: Saving Great Lakes whitefish is a race against time After months spent writing about the looming collapse of whitefish in the lower Great Lakes, Bridge Michigan teamed up with Detroit PBS to tell the story in documentary form. Here’s how to watch.

I'm excited to see more papers on Dreissenid veligers (larvae)! Especially as their veligers may be a major component of zooplankton in some areas of the Great Lakes. Quagga and zebra mussel veligers = junk food for fish aslopubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/...

"[Quagga mussels] are such efficient filter feeders that fewer than every two weeks the entire volume of Lake Michigan is filtered by them, resulting in clear, blue water that’s beautiful but inhospitable for many lake species."
www.wpr.org/environment/...
I’ll be presenting virtually at noon in this session to talk about quagga mussel selective feeding on Lake Erie Microcystis strains

New short film highlights Sault Tribe's connection with #GreatLakes Lake Whitefish and the Tribe's efforts to help rebuild whitefish populations. (the film is linked within the story)
nativenewsonline.net/arts-enterta...
Whitefish population on the verge of collapse #GreatLakes www.jsonline.com/story/news/2...

Round gobies might be small, but these bottom-dwelling invaders are TERRIFYING for our ecosystems. Their voracious appetites and aggressive natures allow them to dominate over native species. #GreatLakes #CleanWater #InvasiveSpecies

Historians race to find Great Lakes shipwrecks before quagga mussels destroy the sites | AP News
apnews.com/article/quag...
A Lake Whitefish and Cisco talk by .bsky.social at the 2024 Great Lakes Fishery Commission Lake Committee meeting.

See what lies below the waves of the Great Lakes. An excellent doc that explores the impact of invasive mussels on the underwater ecosystem. Very nice ROV images of fish, wrecks, bottom habitats, etc.
www.youtube.com/watch?v=udbk...

Delta Ecosystem Threatened by Another Nonnative Mollusk
By Andrew Cohen and Peter Moyle Golden Mussel Limnoperna fortunei. Photo: Great Lakes Echo, Alexander Karatayev. One of the world’s most invasive freshwater mussels has arrived in North Ameri…
californiawaterblog....

Lake whitefish are crashing in parts of Michigan’s Great Lakes.
Invasive mussels are starving their young.
Now, tribes and scientists are racing to revive them, planting eggs in rivers that haven’t seen whitefish spawn in a century.
www.npr.org/2025/04/12/n... #GreatLakes #Michigan
I have learned this is an Asian clam and I am devastated I was super excited to find a mollusc that *isn't* a zebra mussel only to find out this one is also invasive. Protect the Great Lakes 😭

Join us at #IAGLR25 for a screening of the documentary .bsky.social. Featuring cutting-edge underwater drones, filmmakers explore how quadrillions of tiny invasive quagga mussels are re-engineering the ecosystem of the Great Lakes. Tickets:

What’s more Michigan than whitefish? Lake Whitefish are part of our identify in the upper Great Lakes. Their collapse is an economic and cultural problem.
#GreatLakes #Fisheries #PureMichigan
www.bridgemi.com/michigan-env...
The traditional “Can you put Old Bay on zebra mussels infesting the Great Lakes?” Bowl

Reminding me of my old life in a taxonomy lab - zebra mussels are horribly invasive - but their crazy overpopulation was responsible for cleaning up the Great Lakes, grabbing all that nastiness and sequestrring it in a biomass layer at the bottom of the lakes. (Also - this is a crazy cool thread)

From .bsky.social: Lake Superior isn't jammed with the invasive mussels that plague the rest of the Great Lakes. But the lake's tenuous advantages could be falling away, threatening the lake's fishery:

Michigan lawmakers seek $500M to stop mussels, save Great Lakes whitefish US Reps. Debbie Dingell and Tim Walberg are planning legislation to boost research to control invasive mussels, following Bridge Michigan reporting about the impending collapse of whitefish.

Free screening of "All Too Clear" next week! Everyone is invited to see this documentary on how quagga and zebra mussels have transformed Great Lakes food webs, leading to overall decline in whitefish pops Thursday April 9, 530pm, 1101 State Hall, Wayne State University Detroit MI

I'll be on the Q&A panel wearing my "Detroit Mussel" tshirt! Come see "All Too Clear" - a documentary about invasive quagga mussels and how they transformed Lake Michigan Tonight at Wayne State University in Detroit - 530pm in 1101 State Hall

Incredible underwater footage of Lake Huron. Including the devastation wrought by invasive zebra mussels. The fish are drawn to warmer waters created by outfall from a nuclear power plant (the cooling water). There’s no food left for freshwater fish where the zebra mussels invaded.

Join our Invasive Mussel Collaborative for their next webinar on research being done on the zebra mussel invasion front! Register here: invasivemusselcollaborative.net/event/webina... #GreatLakes #invasivespecies

Now available: a recording of our Invasive Mussel Collaborative's recent webinar assessing the risks of zebra mussel establishment along the western United States invasion front invasivemusselcollaborative.net/event/webina... #GreatLakes #invasivespecies

On 7/1, join our Invasive Mussels Collaborative for its webinar "Clash of the Shells" to learn about dreissenid mussel impacts on native unionids! Presenters will include Kennedy Zwarych of @‪mcgill.ca‬ and Alexander Karatayev of Buffalo State. RSVP: ow.ly/vP9f50W2Qgn #GreatLakes
: Quagga mussels, finger-sized mollusks with voracious appetites, have become the dominant invasive species in the lower Great Lakes over the past 30 years, according to biologists.

In most cases eutrophication would not greatly change the rate that wood deteriorates. However, if invasive mussels accompany eutrophication it would be bad. This is happening in the Great lakes, esp where quagga mussels sink their byssal threads into wood, so shipwrecks are deteriorating faster.
Filmmakers capture Great Lakes whitefish in rare spawning event ca.news.yahoo.com/filmmakers-c... via

watching a documentary about the Great Lakes and whitefish i’m gonna have to kick the ass of every single quagga mussel

While at dinner celebration last night, I overheard another table talking about zebra mussels as nasty invaders in the Great Lakes! They were probs actually talking about quagga mussels, but it was refreshing to hear that random people care about the Great Lakes
And accidental semi successes, like the zebra mussels (bad, nasty bastards) helping to clean up Lake Erie. The Great lakes are a treasure, though. I'm a fan.

Invasive quagga mussels are sucking the life out of Lake Michigan, creating a biological desert that has iconic whitefish fighting for survival. Bridge Michigan, which Mott supports, explores the tragic ramifications of a crisis affecting four of the five Great Lakes.

Anyone who loves Michigan's Whitefish will be saddened to know the plight this species is facing.
I grew up eating Smoked Whitefish. It's one of my family's favorite food. "Whitefish have remained a staple in the Great Lakes for millennia, surviving threats that decimated other species."
Study of invasive mussels finds PFAS is nearly everywhere in the Great Lakes www.bridgemi.com/michigan-env... via .com

The damage is multifaceted. Quagga mussels are but one of the culprits. Egan does a great job of explaining how the opening of the St. Lawrence Seaway affected the Great Lakes and still does.

The Great Lakes whitefish—once iconic and abundant—are collapsing. Bridge Michigan and Detroit PBS follow fishers, scientists, and tribal leaders fighting to save a species devastated by invasive mussels. Watch the new Great Lakes Now episode.

Species spotlight for National Invasive Species Awareness Week. Invasive faucet snails are a growing concern in the Champlain Basin and Great Lakes region and can outcompete native snails, clog drain and intake pipes, and spread parasites to birds.
🌍🧪 #NISAW #InvasiveSpecies #Wildlife #Ecology
Goldfish are an invasive species in Canada 🇨🇦, with significant populations found in every province, esp. in urban stormwater ponds, lakes, and the Great Lakes. www.cbc.ca/news/canada/...
The Great Lakes don’t need more invasive species. But ballast water from freighters can move zebra mussels from port to port. Canada is stepping up. U.S. standards still exempt most existing ships.

As scientists work to understand what makes Green Bay unique, the findings will aid recovery efforts throughout the Great Lakes. Michigan biologists have drawn inspiration from Green Bay as they attempt to transplant the state’s whitefish into similar areas.
bridgemi.com/michigan-env...
Turns out that when ships fill their ballast tanks with raw seawater and dump them in the Great Lakes, that water contains a whole lot of invasive organisms. That’s how zebra mussels got here!
At what point did you consider just turning right, driving until you got to Lake Michigan, and then starting a new life as a zebra mussel?

One of my fave NOAA scientists (Ashley Elgin) studies quagga mussels. She is the only person I know who is studying the deep morph quaggas that live in up to 90 meters depth in Lake Michigan! Other NOAA scientists have reported that quaggas are the reason why S Lake MI is void of phytoplankton
The quaggas out-competed the zebra mussels, at least in Lake Michigan and are a serious problem because unlike zebra mussels, quagga can colonize in much deeper water.
Crap. That means we have to keep him I guess we can just throw him in Lake Michigan and let the zebra mussels sort the whole thing out
Zebra Mussels in Lake Michigan might give you a way to show the impact of unintended migration, the water ballast tanks transferred them to this location.

A new technique has been employed to scour invasive zebra mussels off of rock formations at the bottom of Lake Erie, to protect and restore lake trout.
buffalonews.com/news/local/a...

If they were edible or even palatable that'd be one thing but this is another thing all together. FFS why can't we have invasive Lobster or Blue Fin Tuna. Scientists find proof of invasive shrimp in Lake Superior - www.miningjournal.net/news/front-p...

Mollusk 🐌 fans: Dr. Shay Keretz's newest paper on GASTROPODS (🐌🐚) in large rivers is out! #GreatLakes #DetroitRiver

Go for the Great Lakes trifecta with a zebra mussel

Great Lakes whitefish are in danger, and scientists aim to "rewire their brains" by planting their eggs in rivers, using turkey basters, casserole trays and containers resembling giant Lego pieces.
www.bridgemi.com/michigan-env...
Looking forward to the release of "All too clear" about the impact of invasive zebra and quagga mussels on fish in the Great Lakes. stockeycentre.com/event/all-to...
Getting some payback for the zebra mussels in the Great Lakes. We should send a team of walleye over somewhere and really fuck em up.
Zebra mussels and Quagga mussels top the list as of most impactful invasive species in the Great Lakes.

Always happy to repost about the welfare of Animals! The zebra mussels were introduced when ships coming from Europe emptied their ballast water into the Great Lakes. Granted, it's not direct human action, it's still human ignorance of the planet and other species' potential interactions, IMO.
Iconic whitefish on edge of collapse as Great Lakes biodiversity crisis deepens www.bridgemi.com/michigan-env... via .com
Iconic whitefish on edge of collapse as Great Lakes biodiversity crisis deepens www.bridgemi.com/michigan-env...

Invasive Mussels Threaten Whitefish Populations Across Great Lakes
"[Quagga mussels] filter phytoplankton—the base of the aquatic food chain—directly from the water. That limits food availability for zooplankton, which are a critical food source for young whitefish."
wzmq19.com/news/350680/...

I was wondering why I was no longer hearing about the zebra mussels! I did not know about these things. There ought to be a huge fine and complete banning for shipping companies that do stuff like this. Especially in our Great Lakes. I worry about Superior. Thanks for educating me on this. 👍

As invasive mussels boomed in the Great Lakes, lake whitefish populations dwindled. The Spawning Whitefish and Invasive Mussel (SWIM) Project from .bsky.social is on a 5-year mission to tease out how these two species are linked. Check out their quick animated video: buff.ly/Jszuxsr

Word of the Hour: Mysis - A genus of small schizopod shrimps found both in fresh and salt water; the opossum shrimps. One species inhabits the Great Lakes of North America and is largely eaten by the whitefish. The marine species form part of the food of right whales. #WordOfTheHour #WordOfTheDay
Just went down a fascinating rabbit hole. Historians race to find Great Lakes shipwrecks before quagga mussels destroy the sites apnews.com/article/quag...
As invasive mussels ravage the Great Lakes, Lake Superior is the last holdout: thenarwhal.ca/lake-superio...

“But by removing the plankton, quaggas also remove the bottom of the food chain, meaning that important whitefish populations are collapsing. There are now literally quadrillions of the mollusks blanketing most of the bottom of the lower Great Lakes.” www.wpr.org/environment/...

“An invasive species of mussel is revealing the shipwrecks of the US Great Lakes, while also damaging local ecosystems. The quagga mussel came to North America from Europe in the 1980s in ships’ ballast. They filter plankton from the water, making it clearer and rendering wrecks visible.”
Invasive Zebra snails are wiping out Great Lakes Whitefish. share.google/uH4PK1oP0U32...

Ugh no, first off zebra mussels are mollusks, secondly, it isn't any spice that people have used to paint boat bottoms, it is hot peppers specifically. Lastly, there is an even worse invader in the Great Lakes called quagga mussels and they are devastating the lakes.
"Those works dive deep into how invasive mussels in the Great Lakes are gobbling up essential nutrients and devastating organisms, from plankton to whitefish."
If you know anything about zebra mussels, the great lakes would probably scoop you up… maybe a fairy shrimp can magically teleport zebra mussels…
Michigan lawmakers seek $500M to stop mussels, save Great Lakes whitefish www.bridgedetroit.com/michigan-law...
Neat (and terrifying) how you can see the quagga winning out over the zebra mussel in the Great Lakes already. Lake Michigan is pretty clear there...

“Quagga mussels invaded the US’s Great Lakes in 1989 and are now the dominant life form, making up more than 99% of all invertebrate biomass in some lakes” Seems to be no solution that can outrun the fast-breeding quaggas

Yeah, they spread throughout the Great Lakes - even the Canadian parts. (note to .com: there's only 1 US Great Lake. The other 4 are jointly owned by Canada and the US.) I'm curious to know how the new infrastructure will be mussel-resistant.

🏞️ Invasive mussels are back in the news, as their long-documented impacts continue to threaten Great Lakes whitefish on both sides of the U.S.–Canada border: 🔗 www.ecosystemscience.ca/news/jko2rpj...

Quagga mussels - the animal in my profile pic - have caused ecosystem shifts in 4/5 Laurentian Great Lakes They're like kobolds - individually they are tiny and weak but are able to overwhelm the environment with their sheer numbers

“whitefish recruitment has crashed by 70–80 percent since the late 1990s in Lake Michigan and Lake Huron due to the combined effects of mussel-driven food scarcity and declining winter ice cover” Tribes move whitefish upriver in bid to revive Great Lakes runs www.mlive.com/environment/...

Quagga mussels are destroying the food chain in the main lakes and they still allow ballast dumping so they keep moving mussels to new areas. Our Great Lakes shipping companies are greedy and don't care that their actions are wiping out millions of fish.

Some of those shells were of the genus Dreissena and an entire FBI team analyzed whether they were invasive Great Lakes zebra mussels or whether Comey had traveled to China and illegally imported them for the express purpose of sea shell threat delivery

Everyone is trying to keep those fucking assholes from taking over the Great Lakes-not just mariners and freshwater biologists. ALL of us who live around here are. Those things are a fucking scourge, much like Zebra mussels.

The stupidity of this post. Cormorants are an invasive species on the Great Lakes and inland waterways. There are no factory ships on any of them. While overfishing is a serious ocean problem, being unfactual doesn't help. Even a little bit.
Cormorants are destroying native fishstocks and birds.

Are these zebra mussels on this boulder that was pulled from Lake Michigan? I am at South Shore Park in Milwaukee, where the existing park beach is being moved to the south as a way to deal with E. coli contamination at its current location.
Lake Michigan has been battling zebra mussels for 30 years.Mitigations are limited. www.michigan.gov/-/media/Proj...

I detest zebra mussels with a passion -_- Istg you walk on any beach near lake Michigan and they shred your feet the tide was out today and it was literally a mile of nothing but piles of crushed zebra mussels for half a mile at the watermark
We got zebra mussels under control but the quagga mussels love deep water and they re covering the bottom in many areas. They are devastating to the lakes, especially Lake Michigan.

Commercial harvest of lake whitefish, a fish fry favorite, from Lake Michigan has declined nearly 90 percent in the last quarter-century. They are the victims of warming temperatures and invasive mussels, and they really could disappear. www.wpr.org/news/decline...

Lake Superior averages about 12-13mg/L [Ca], which is too low for zebra & quagga mussels to grow and reproduce. There is no evidence that they have adapted to other low-calcium habitats. Perhaps there are localized areas within the lake basin that have sufficient [Ca].
